questions.question: List and explain any five separators used in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Parentheses () - method parameters and grouping expressions (e.g., main(String[] args))
Braces {} - defining blocks of code (e.g., class definitions, method bodies)
Square brackets [] - array declarations and element access (e.g., int[10] arr, arr[0])
Semicolon ; - statement terminator (e.g., int x = 5;)
Comma , - separating parameters and variables (e.g., method(a, b))

questions.question: What is the difference between square brackets [] used in array declaration versus array access?
keyPoints: In declaration: int[] arr - brackets appear after type
In access: arr[5] - brackets appear after variable name with index
The position differs but the separator is the same
Both indicate array but serve different syntactic purposes

questions.question: Explain the role of the period (.) operator in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Package separator: java.util.Scanner separates package hierarchy
Member access: object.method() or Class.staticField accesses class members
Creates fully qualified names for classes

questions.question: What are lambda expressions and which separators are used in them?
keyPoints: Lambda expressions are anonymous functions introduced in Java 8
Arrow operator (->) separates parameters from body
Double colon (::) is used for method references
Example: (a, b) -> a + b or String::toUpperCase

questions.question: What is varargs and which separator is used to define it?
keyPoints: Varargs (variable-length arguments) allows methods to accept variable number of arguments
Ellipsis (...) separator is used before the parameter name
Example: void method(int... numbers)
Can be used with zero or more arguments

questions.question: Differentiate between separators and operators in Java.
keyPoints: Separators define code structure and boundaries (punctuation)
Operators perform operations on values/variables
Separators include (), {}, [], ;, ,, ., :, ->, ::
Operators include +, -, *, /, =, ==, etc.

questions.question: Explain the uses of colon (:) separator in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Ternary operator: condition ? expr1 : expr2
Enhanced for-loop: for(type item : collection)
Labeled statements: label: statement
Separates different parts in these constructs

questions.question: Explain all the separators in Java with their purposes and provide code examples for each.
keyPoints: Parentheses (): method parameters, grouping expressions, control structures
Braces {}: class definitions, method bodies, blocks, array initialization
Square brackets []: array declarations and element access
Semicolon ;: statement terminator
Comma ,: separating method parameters, variables, array elements
Period .: package separator, accessing class members
Colon : : ternary operator, enhanced for-loop, labels
Arrow ->: lambda expressions (Java 8+)
Double colon ::: method references (Java 8+)
Ellipsis ...: varargs (variable-length arguments)

questions.question: Write a Java program demonstrating the use of different separators in a single program. Include examples of method calls, arrays, and lambda expressions.
keyPoints: Method definition with parentheses and braces
Array declaration with square brackets
Method call with parentheses and comma-separated arguments
Semicolons terminating statements
Lambda expression with arrow operator
Method reference with double colon operator
Varargs with ellipsis

questions.question: Discuss how the meaning of separators changes in different contexts in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Square brackets: declaration (int[] arr) vs access (arr[0])
Parentheses: method call vs grouping expressions
Curly braces: class body, method body, array init, static block
Colon: ternary operator vs enhanced for-loop vs label
Period: package separator vs member access

questions.question: Explain the evolution of Java separators from Java 1 to Java 8. Which new separators were introduced and why?
keyPoints: Java 1-7: Basic separators (), {}, [], ;, ,, ., :
Java 8 introduced: Arrow operator (->) for lambda expressions
Java 8 introduced: Double colon (::) for method references
These additions supported functional programming features
Ellipsis existed from early versions for varargs
